description:
You are a build-and-release engineer who must produce a quick performance datapoint for management.

1. A build artifact already exists at  
   /home/user/artifacts/build_v1.2.3.tar

2. Benchmark requirement  
   • You must measure how long it takes to compress that artifact with gzip in its default configuration.  
   • Use only a **single terminal command** to perform both the compression and the timing.  
   • Do not delete or alter the original TAR file; the compressed file (*.gz) may remain.  

3. Result reporting  
   • Write the timing result to the file  
     /home/user/benchmark/compress_benchmark.log  
     (create the benchmark directory if it is missing).  
   • The log must contain exactly **one line** and **one line only**.  
   • Line format – it must match this pattern precisely:  

     YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ|wall_seconds:SSS.SSS  

     where  
       – the left side is the current timestamp in **UTC** using ISO-8601 basic form and ending with the literal letter Z, e.g. 2025-04-09T19:34:21Z  
       – the right side is the wall-clock time in seconds, expressed with exactly three digits after the decimal point (e.g. 0.015 or 12.347).  
   • No extra spaces, no additional lines, no headers.

A correct example (do **not** copy these values verbatim):

2025-04-09T19:34:21Z|wall_seconds:0.015

The automated grader will verify:

• the file exists at the exact path given;  
• it contains one and only one line;  
• the line conforms character-for-character to the pattern above.

Produce that log file. No further output is required.
truth:
Initial filesystem state that exists before the agent begins:

1. Directory /home/user/artifacts/ already present with permissions 755.  
2. File /home/user/artifacts/build_v1.2.3.tar  
   • size ≈ 11 KB  
   • plain text content: “Hello Build Engineer\n” repeated ~700 times (exact content not important for grading).  
   • permissions 644.

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

1. Directory /home/user/benchmark/ (permissions 755, can be newly created).  
2. File /home/user/benchmark/compress_benchmark.log  
   • permissions 644.  
   • single-line content matching the regular expression  

   ^[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z\|wall_seconds:[0-9]+\.[0-9]{3}$  

   (timestamp and numeric value may vary).  
   • No trailing newline is enforced but allowed.

3. Optional by-product: /home/user/artifacts/build_v1.2.3.tar.gz (size may vary); presence not required for grading.

The test harness will:

1. Assert the benchmark log exists at the exact path.  
2. Read its only line; ensure it matches the regex above.  
3. Fail if extra lines, spaces, or files are misplaced.

No root privileges are needed; default user account “user” suffices.
